site.name: Regularízate.es
site.tagline: Your guide to regularizing your status in Spain
site.description: Free step-by-step guide for immigration regularization in Spain.

countdown.closed: The extraordinary regularization has closed. See the ordinary residency routes.
countdown.cta: In Spain before January 1, 2026? Start here

landing.hero_title: Want to regularize your immigration status in Spain?
landing.hero_subtitle: Free step-by-step guide. No legal jargon.
landing.card_extraordinary_title: I've lived in Spain for years, undocumented
landing.card_extraordinary_desc: You may apply for extraordinary regularization if you've been here since before January 1, 2026. Deadline: June 30, 2026.
landing.card_pi_title: I applied for asylum, denied or still waiting
landing.card_pi_desc: If you applied for international protection before January 1, 2026, there's a specific regularization route.
landing.card_ordinary_title: I've been here 2+ years : ordinary residency route
landing.card_ordinary_desc: Social, sociolaboral, family, socioformative, or second chance route. No deadline.
landing.disclaimer_short: This site is informational only. For legal advice, consult a lawyer specializing in immigration law.
landing.how_1_desc: 3 quick questions to confirm you meet the requirements.
landing.how_2_desc: Complete list with where to get each document. Copyable in one click.
landing.how_3_desc: ~10.72 € : step by step to generate Form 790-052.
landing.how_4_desc: Online (Mercurio) or at one of the 436 in-person points.
landing.dates_close_desc: Last chance for EX-32 (DA 21ª) and EX-31 (DA 20ª). After that, only the ordinary residency routes.
landing.dates_permanent_desc: 5 pathways (second chance, sociolaboral, social, socioformative, family) remain valid indefinitely.

tunnel.not_eligible: This route doesn't apply to your case
tunnel.not_eligible_desc: Based on your answers, this regularization route isn't right for you. Check the other options.

extraordinary.title: Extraordinary Regularization (DA 21ª)
extraordinary.subtitle: Royal Decree 316/2026 : Form EX-32
extraordinary.legal_basis: Additional Provision Twenty-First of RD 1155/2024, introduced by RD 316/2026
extraordinary.deadline_note: This route closes June 30, 2026. No extension is planned.
extraordinary.work_auth_note: New: from the moment your application is admitted, you receive provisional authorization to work (employed and self-employed).
extraordinary.quiz_q2: Have you been in Spain since before January 1, 2026?
extraordinary.quiz_q3: Do you currently have a valid residence permit or a pending application?
extraordinary.quiz_q3_note: If you have a valid permit or pending application, this route doesn't apply.
extraordinary.quiz_q4: Do you have a criminal record in Spain or your home country?
extraordinary.cond_continuity: Continuous presence of at least 5 months before applying
extraordinary.cond_no_permit: No valid residence permit or pending application
extraordinary.validity: 1-year authorization, renewable. Equivalent to social residency for exceptional circumstances.
extraordinary.doc_form: Form EX-32 properly completed and signed
extraordinary.doc_passport: Valid full passport (at least 4 months remaining)
extraordinary.doc_passport_where: Issued by your consulate or home country
extraordinary.doc_empadronamiento_where: At your local town hall (padrón municipal service)
extraordinary.doc_antecedentes_es_where: Ministry of Justice : online or in-person
extraordinary.doc_antecedentes_origen: Criminal background certificate from home country (apostille + certified translation)
extraordinary.doc_antecedentes_origen_where: Consulate or authorities of your home country. Must be apostilled and translated by a certified translator.
extraordinary.doc_tasa: Proof of payment for Form 790 code 052
extraordinary.doc_tasa_where: Generate at sede.administracionespublicas.gob.es, pay at partner bank or online
extraordinary.doc_ties: Supporting documents for ties or situation (as applicable: work contract, vulnerability report, etc.)
extraordinary.step4_tasa_desc: The fee for authorization for exceptional circumstances is approximately €10.72. The exact amount appears on the generated form.
extraordinary.step4_tasa_steps: How to generate and pay the fee:
extraordinary.step4_tasa_1: Go to the official generator at sede.administracionespublicas.gob.es
extraordinary.step4_tasa_3: Choose: 'Autorización de residencia temporal por circunstancias excepcionales'
extraordinary.step4_tasa_4: Fill in your details and generate the form
extraordinary.step4_tasa_5: Pay at a partner bank or via the AEAT online gateway
extraordinary.step4_tasa_warning: Keep the NRC receipt if you pay online. Without it, your application may be rejected.
extraordinary.step5_online_desc: You can submit your application online via Plataforma Mercurio with Cl@ve or digital certificate.
extraordinary.step5_presencial_desc: 437 in-person offices: 371 Correos post offices, 60 Social Security offices, and 5 Immigration offices (Madrid, Alicante, Valencia, Almería, Murcia).
extraordinary.step5_appointment: Prior appointment is mandatory. Book via the ministry website, online form, or call 060.
extraordinary.step6_work_auth: While waiting: provisional work authorization from the moment of admission
extraordinary.step6_tie_title: Once your authorization is granted: the TIE
extraordinary.step6_tie_desc: The TIE (Tarjeta de Identidad de Extranjero) is your physical residence document.
extraordinary.step6_tie_1: Make an appointment at your local police station (Provincial Immigration Brigade)
extraordinary.step6_tie_2: Pay Form 790 code 012 (~€16.08) : TIE issuance fee
extraordinary.step6_tie_3: Bring: granted authorization, passport, photo, fee 012 receipt
extraordinary.step6_tie_4: Your fingerprints and photo will be taken
extraordinary.step6_tie_5: Pick up TIE at the same police station (~4-6 weeks)
extraordinary.cond_intro: Check each requirement you meet. You need all of them to apply.
extraordinary.docs_intro: Complete list of what you need to submit.
extraordinary.complete_desc: You have everything to start your process. The deadline is 30 June 2026.
extraordinary.apostilla_warning: Foreign criminal records must be apostilled and have a sworn translation.
extraordinary.cond_link: Prove at least one link: work (contract), family (minor children or ascendants), or social vulnerability
extraordinary.cond_no_ukraine: Not holding temporary protection granted under EU Decision 2022/382 (Ukraine)

pi.title: Residency for International Protection (DA 20ª)
pi.subtitle: Royal Decree 316/2026 : Form EX-31
pi.legal_basis: Additional Provision Twentieth of RD 1155/2024, introduced by RD 316/2026
pi.quiz_q2: Did you apply for international protection (asylum) before January 1, 2026?
pi.quiz_q3: Was your application denied, withdrawn, or still pending without final resolution?
pi.quiz_q4: Do you have serious criminal convictions in Spain?
pi.cond_asylum_request: International protection application submitted before January 1, 2026
pi.cond_status: Application denied, withdrawn, or still being processed
pi.cond_continuity: Continuous presence of at least 5 months before applying
pi.doc_form_label: EX-31 form duly completed and signed
pi.cond_no_permit: No other valid residence permit or pending application (other than the international protection request)
pi.warning_parallel: 💡 If your PI request is still pending (no decision yet): you can apply for EX-31. If granted, your PI procedure will be automatically archived. If you have an active PI judicial appeal, consult a professional before applying.
pi.cond_no_ukraine: Not holding temporary protection granted under EU Decision 2022/382 (Ukraine)

ordinary.title: Ordinary Regularization
ordinary.subtitle: 5 permanent routes : Form EX-07
ordinary.no_deadline: No deadline : these routes are permanent
ordinary.segunda_oportunidad_desc: You had a residence permit in the last 2 years, but didn't renew it for reasons unrelated to public order/security/health.
ordinary.sociolaboral_desc: 2 years of presence + employment contract of at least 20h/week at SMI (€1,221/month in 2026).
ordinary.social_desc: 2 years of presence + family ties with income ≥ 100% IPREM (€600/month), or a social integration report from the Autonomous Community.
ordinary.socioformativo_desc: 2 years of presence + enrollment in a certified vocational training program. Work limited to 30h/week during training.
ordinary.familiar_desc: No minimum presence required. Family tie with a Spanish authorization holder or Spanish child.
ordinary.absences_note: Maximum absences: 90 days over the required 2 years of presence
ordinary.submit_desc: Immigration office in your province. Appointment required.
ordinary.note_1: Foreign criminal records: apostilled + sworn translation.
ordinary.note_2: Maximum absences over the 2-year period: 90 days.
ordinary.note_3: Negative silence: 3 months without response = tacit refusal.
ordinary.doc_antecedentes: Criminal record (Spain + origin, apostilled + sworn translation)
ordinary.doc_social_ties: Family ties or social integration report from the autonomous community
ordinary.doc_prev_permit: Documentation of previous permit and reasons for non-renewal
ordinary.cond_no_pi: Not be an international protection applicant at the time of application or during its processing
ordinary.sociolaboral_pi_note: Note: if you were a PI applicant, the time during its processing does not count toward the required 2 years of presence.
ordinary.sociolaboral_multi_contract: Multiple contracts can be combined to reach 20h/week: consecutive seasonal contracts with different employers, or simultaneous part-time jobs.
ordinary.sociolaboral_ss_registration: Once the authorization is granted, you must register with Social Security within 1 month of notification. Without this step, the authorization has no effect.
ordinary.sociolaboral_qualification: For regulated professions in Spain (doctor, lawyer, architect…): you must provide the official recognition or equivalence of your qualification.

map.title: Where to submit your application
map.subtitle: 436 in-person locations for extraordinary regularization
map.geoloc_error_permission: You denied location access. Enable it in your browser settings.
map.geoloc_error_timeout: Location request timed out. Try again.
map.no_results: No offices found with current filters.
map.appointment_channel: Book via the ministry website, online form, or call 060

glossary.nie: Número de Identificación de Extranjero : your unique ID number in Spain
glossary.tie: Tarjeta de Identidad de Extranjero : physical residence card
glossary.arraigo: Rootedness. In immigration law, it refers to your demonstrable connection to Spain (time of residence, work, family, etc.)
glossary.empadronamiento: Municipal residents register. The certificate proves how long you've been registered in a municipality.
glossary.apostilla: Official stamp that validates foreign documents for use in Spain. Equivalent to simplified legalization.
glossary.traduccion_jurada: Official translation by a translator certified by the Spanish Ministry of Foreign Affairs
glossary.tasa: Official fee paid to the State for processing an administrative application
glossary.admision_tramite: Official confirmation that your file has been received and processing has begun
glossary.nrc: Full Reference Number : code confirming online fee payment
glossary.iprem: Public Income Indicator for Multiple Effects : minimum income reference. In 2026: €600/month
glossary.mercurio: Ministry's online platform for submitting immigration applications
glossary.cita_previa: Fixed-time appointment at an office. Required for in-person submission.
glossary.circunstancias_excepcionales: Legal category covering regularization routes. Allows obtaining residence outside the ordinary routes.
glossary.da_21: Additional Provision Twenty-First of RD 1155/2024, introduced by RD 316/2026. Governs extraordinary regularization.
glossary.da_20: Additional Provision 20ª of RD 1155/2024, introduced by RD 316/2026. Governs residency for international protection.
glossary.ccaa: Autonomous Community : each of Spain's 17 autonomous regions
glossary.ex_07: Official form for temporary residence for exceptional circumstances (ordinary regularization routes)
glossary.ex_31: Official form for residency for international protection (DA 20ª, RD 316/2026)
glossary.ex_32: Official form for extraordinary regularization (DA 21ª, RD 316/2026)

footer.disclaimer: This site is purely informational. It does not constitute legal advice. For legal consultation, contact a lawyer specializing in immigration law.
footer.rights: Information verified May 2026. Data may change.
